task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. Empty lists, one-element lists, and two-element lists return None. When all values are equal, one of them still drops as the minimum and one as the maximum, and the mean of the rest equals that same value. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function meets all the requirements but does not explicitly handle an edge case for when all values are equal.
trace:
**
The function `trimmed_mean` first checks if the list has fewer than three elements. If so, it returns `None` because trimming one minimum and one maximum is not possible without violating the condition of having at least two elements left to average. For valid lists, the function removes the first occurrence of both the minimum and maximum values using `remove()` method. Finally, it calculates and returns the mean of the remaining numbers as a float. This approach ensures that the function handles edge cases like empty lists, one-element lists, and two-element lists correctly while performing the core task efficiently.
